20-9-9.kjv: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.

20-9-11.web: For by me your days will be multiplied. The years of your life will be increased.
20-9-11.kjv: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.

20-10-11.web: The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
20-10-11.kjv: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

20-10-13.web: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has discernment, but a rod is for the back of him who is void of understanding.
20-10-13.kjv: In the lips of him that hath understanding wisdom is found: but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.

20-11-13.web: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence, but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.
20-11-13.kjv: A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter.

20-11-15.web: He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it, but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.
20-11-15.kjv: He that is surety for a stranger shall smart for it: and he that hateth suretiship is sure.

20-12-24.web: The hands of the diligent ones shall rule, but laziness ends in slave labor.
20-12-24.kjv: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.

20-12-26.web: A righteous person is cautious in friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
20-12-26.kjv: The righteous is more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.

20-14-11.web: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
20-14-11.kjv: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.

20-14-13.web: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.
20-14-13.kjv: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness.

20-15-2.web: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge, but the mouth of fools gush out folly.
20-15-2.kjv: The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fools poureth out foolishness.

20-16-8.web: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues with injustice.
20-16-8.kjv: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.

20-16-2.web: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but Yahweh weighs the motives.
20-16-2.kjv: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the LORD weigheth the spirits.

20-16-10.web: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king. He shall not betray his mouth.
20-16-10.kjv: A divine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.

20-16-4.web: Yahweh has made everything for its own end — yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.
20-16-4.kjv: The LORD hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.

20-17-16.kjv: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?

20-17-18.kjv: A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.

20-18-11.kjv: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit.
